key_themes: Strong Q2 earnings beats, record bookings, and AI momentum with strategic acquisitions
Full-year revenue guidance light versus consensus, contributing to post-update sell-off
Attractive depressed valuation but near-term growth/macro concerns and recent downgrade
summary: Accenture reported solid Q2 FY2026 results with revenue of $18B (4% LC growth, beat), EPS $2.93 (beat), record $22.1B bookings, margin expansion, and strong FCF; it raised the low end of FY guidance, highlighted doubling AI-related activity, and announced AI/data acquisitions. However, the full-year revenue range came in below street expectations, sparking negative price reaction, a Seeking Alpha downgrade to Hold on slowing momentum/mixed pricing, and ongoing macro/IT spending worries—despite the stock now trading at a decade-low multiple with consensus Moderate Buy targets ~25-30% higher around $250. Biggest risk is persistent softness in enterprise spending or bookings that limits AI-driven acceleration.[[1]](https://newsroom.accenture.com/news/2026/accenture-reports-second-quarter-fiscal-2026-results)[[2]](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/accenture-nyseacn-releases-fy-2026-earnings-guidance-2026-04-09/)[[3]](https://seekingalpha.com/article/4890718-accenture-stock-downgrade-to-hold-as-near-term-growth-acceleration-seems-unlikely)

recent_posts.highlight: Q2 beat on rev/EPS, record $22.1B bookings, AI doubling, raised FY growth floor; stock up 5% in tough market.
Did AI kill consulting? ACN trading at lowest valuation in over a decade (EV/EBIT 10.6x).
IT stocks ready to crash; ACN down 9% on news Anthropic AI automation tool could eat into data/info services biz.
Raised FCF guidance, $1.6B in strategic AI acquisitions, maintained outlook; trading at ~15x P/E with 24% ROE — AI pivot real but priced like a cyclical.
